# portkey_api_key (Resource)

Manages a Portkey API Key.

API Key Types:
- Admin API Key (type="organisation", sub_type="service"): Access to Admin APIs for organization management
- Workspace Service Key (type="workspace", sub_type="service"): Workspace-scoped service access
- Workspace User Key (type="workspace", sub_type="user"): User-specific workspace access (requires user_id)

~> **Note:** Removing `usage_limits` or `rate_limits` from your Terraform config will clear them on the Portkey backend.

## Example Usage

### Basic Workspace API Key

```terraform
resource "portkey_api_key" "basic" {
  name         = "My Service Key"
  type         = "workspace"
  sub_type     = "service"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id

  scopes = [
    "completions.write",
    "providers.read",
    "providers.list",
  ]
}
```

### With Usage Limits and Rate Limits

```terraform
resource "portkey_api_key" "with_limits" {
  name         = "Budget-Controlled Key"
  type         = "workspace"
  sub_type     = "service"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id

  scopes = ["completions.write", "providers.list"]

  usage_limits = {
    credit_limit    = 500
    alert_threshold = 400
    periodic_reset  = "monthly"
  }

  rate_limits = [{
    type  = "requests"
    unit  = "rpm"
    value = 1000
  }]
}
```

To clear limits, simply remove the `usage_limits` or `rate_limits` blocks from your config and re-apply.

### Bound to a Config (with optional override control)

```terraform
resource "portkey_config" "routing" {
  name         = "My Routing Config"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id
  config       = jsonencode({ retry = { attempts = 3 } })
}

resource "portkey_api_key" "locked" {
  name                  = "Config-Locked Key"
  type                  = "workspace"
  sub_type              = "service"
  workspace_id          = portkey_workspace.dev.id
  scopes                = ["completions.write"]
  config_id             = portkey_config.routing.id
  allow_config_override = false
}
```

Setting `allow_config_override = true` lets callers supply their own config at request time. On create, omitting both `config_id` and `allow_config_override` creates an unbound key (no default config). On updates, omitting these arguments does not unbind an existing key; the provider preserves the current `config_id`/`allow_config_override` binding unless you change it explicitly.

### With Key Expiry and Rotation Policy

```terraform
resource "portkey_api_key" "rotating" {
  name         = "Short-Lived Service Key"
  type         = "workspace"
  sub_type     = "service"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id
  scopes       = ["completions.write", "providers.list"]

  # Expires on this date. Can also be updated after creation.
  expires_at = "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z"

  # Portkey will automatically rotate this key monthly.
  rotation_policy = {
    rotation_period          = "monthly"
    key_transition_period_ms = 3600000 # 1 hour overlap
  }
}
```

`expires_at` can be set at creation time and updated later. Omit for a non-expiring key. Note: to **clear** an existing `expires_at` value via Terraform, set it explicitly to an empty string or use the Portkey API directly (Terraform cannot distinguish "omitted" from "set to null" for `Optional+Computed` attributes).

### Reset Usage Counters

```terraform
resource "portkey_api_key" "with_reset" {
  name         = "My Service Key"
  type         = "workspace"
  sub_type     = "service"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id
  scopes       = ["completions.write"]

  usage_limits = {
    credit_limit   = 1000
    periodic_reset = "monthly"
  }

  # Set to true to trigger an immediate usage counter reset on the next apply.
  # Remove this line (or set to false) to stop triggering resets.
  reset_usage = true
}
```

`reset_usage` is a **write-only trigger**: it is never persisted in state. Setting it to `true` sends `reset_usage: true` to the API and immediately resets the key's usage counters. After the apply, state stores `null` for this field — if you keep `reset_usage = true` in your config, the next `terraform plan` will show it as a pending change, which will trigger another reset on the next `apply`. Remove the line (or set `reset_usage = false`) once the reset is done.

### On-Demand Key Rotation

```terraform
resource "portkey_api_key" "rotatable" {
  name         = "Rotatable Service Key"
  type         = "workspace"
  sub_type     = "service"
  workspace_id = portkey_workspace.dev.id
  scopes       = ["completions.write"]

  # Bump this string (any new value: a date, an integer, a hash) to force
  # an immediate rotation on the next apply via POST /api-keys/{id}/rotate.
  rotate_trigger = "v1"

  # Optional: how long the previous key keeps working after a rotation.
  # Minimum 1800000 (30 minutes) per the Portkey API spec.
  rotate_transition_period_ms = 3600000
}
```

`rotate_trigger` is a **change-detected trigger**: rotation fires only when its value changes between two applies (including the first time you add it to an existing key). Bumping the value during an apply calls the rotate endpoint, replaces `key` in state with the newly-issued value, and populates `key_transition_expires_at` with the API-returned cut-off for the previous key.

* Setting `rotate_trigger` on a brand-new resource does **not** rotate the key — the key is freshly issued already; the value is just recorded in state for future bumps.
* Removing `rotate_trigger` does **not** rotate the key.
* `rotate_transition_period_ms` is consulted only when `rotate_trigger` fires and is independent of `rotation_policy.key_transition_period_ms` (which governs scheduled rotations).
* **Do not** wire `rotate_trigger = timestamp()` (or any other always-changing expression) — that would rotate the key on every apply and may break consumers still holding the previous value.

#### Required Admin Key Scope

The calling Admin API Key needs the matching `*_api_keys.update` scope:

| Key being rotated                                     | Required Admin scope                       |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `type = "organisation"`, `sub_type = "service"`       | `organisation_service_api_keys.update`     |
| `type = "workspace"`, `sub_type = "service"`          | `workspace_service_api_keys.update`        |
| `type = "workspace"`, `sub_type = "user"`             | `workspace_user_api_keys.update`           |

A 403 (`AB03`) from `/api-keys/{id}/rotate` almost always means this scope is missing. Grant it under **Settings → API Keys** in the Portkey dashboard.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable name for the API key.
- `sub_type` (String) Sub-type of API key: 'service' (machine-to-machine) or 'user' (user-specific, requires user_id).
- `type` (String) Type of API key: 'organisation' (for Admin API keys) or 'workspace' (for workspace-scoped keys).

### Optional

- `alert_emails` (List of String) List of email addresses to receive alerts related to this API key's usage.
- `allow_config_override` (Boolean) Controls whether callers using this API key can override the bound `config_id` at request time. Only meaningful when `config_id` is set. Set to `false` to lock callers to the bound config; set to `true` (default) to allow per-request config overrides. Once set, clearing this field in Terraform will not remove it from the API — use the Portkey API directly to unset it.
- `config_id` (String) ID of the Portkey config to bind as the default config for all requests made using this API key. When set, every request using this key will apply this config by default. Once set, clearing this field in Terraform will not remove the binding from the API — use the Portkey API directly to unset it.
- `description` (String) Optional description of the API key.
- `expires_at` (String) RFC3339 datetime when this API key expires (e.g. `"2026-12-31T23:59:59Z"`). Can be set on create or updated later. Omit for a non-expiring key. To clear an existing expiry, use the Portkey API directly.
- `reset_usage` (Boolean) Write-only trigger. Set to `true` to immediately reset this key's usage counters on the next apply. After the reset is performed, state is set back to `null`. This is never read back from the API.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Custom metadata to attach to the API key. This metadata will be included with every request made using this key. Useful for tracking, observability, and identifying services. Example: `{"_user": "service-name", "service_uuid": "abc123"}`
- `rate_limits` (Attributes List) Rate limits for this API key. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rate_limits))
- `rotate_trigger` (String) Change-detected trigger for on-demand key rotation (`POST /api-keys/{id}/rotate`). Rotation fires when this string changes between applies, replacing `key` and populating `key_transition_expires_at`. Setting it on a new resource does not rotate; removing it does not rotate. See the *On-Demand Key Rotation* example for the required Admin Key scope.
- `rotate_transition_period_ms` (Number) Optional transition period (in milliseconds) applied the next time `rotate_trigger` fires. Minimum 1800000 (30 minutes). Independent of `rotation_policy.key_transition_period_ms`.
- `rotation_policy` (Attributes) Automatic key rotation policy. When set, Portkey will rotate this key on the configured schedule. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rotation_policy))
- `scopes` (List of String) List of permission scopes for this API key.
- `usage_limits` (Attributes) Usage limits for this API key. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--usage_limits))
- `user_id` (String) User ID for user-type keys. Required when sub_type is 'user'.
- `workspace_id` (String) Workspace ID. Required for workspace API keys (type='workspace'). Not used for Admin API keys.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the API key was created.
- `id` (String) API Key identifier (UUID).
- `key` (String, Sensitive) The actual API key value. Returned on creation and on every on-demand rotation; otherwise preserved from prior state.
- `key_transition_expires_at` (String) Timestamp at which the previous key value stops being accepted after the most recent on-demand rotation. Null until `rotate_trigger` has fired at least once.
- `last_reset_at` (String) Timestamp when this API key's usage counters were last reset. Managed by the API.
- `organisation_id` (String) Organisation ID this key belongs to.
- `status` (String) Status of the API key (active, exhausted).
- `updated_at` (String) Timestamp when the API key was last updated.

<a id="nestedatt--rate_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rate_limits`

Required:

- `type` (String) Type of rate limit: `requests` or `tokens`.
- `unit` (String) Rate limit unit: `rpm` (per minute), `rph` (per hour), `rpd` (per day), `rps` (per second), `rpw` (per week).
- `value` (Number) The rate limit value.


<a id="nestedatt--rotation_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rotation_policy`

Optional:

- `rotation_period` (String) How often to rotate the key: `weekly` or `monthly`.
- `next_rotation_at` (String) ISO8601 datetime of the next scheduled rotation. Computed by the API; can be overridden.
- `key_transition_period_ms` (Number) How long (in milliseconds) both the old and new keys remain valid after rotation. Minimum 1800000 (30 minutes).


<a id="nestedatt--usage_limits"></a>
### Nested Schema for `usage_limits`

Optional:

- `type` (String) Limit type: `tokens` (cap on token consumption) or `cost` (cap on dollar spend).
- `credit_limit` (Number) The credit limit value. Required when `usage_limits` is set.
- `alert_threshold` (Number) Alert threshold. Triggers email notification when usage reaches this value.
- `periodic_reset` (String) When to reset the usage counter: `monthly` or `weekly`.
- `periodic_reset_days` (Number) Custom reset interval in days (1–365). Alternative to `periodic_reset` for non-standard cadences.
- `next_usage_reset_at` (String) ISO8601 datetime for the next scheduled usage reset. Optional override; computed by the API when `periodic_reset` is set.
